title: Watch Arbitrage Tracker — Input
description: Track Patek/Rolex/AP price spreads across 6 marketplaces. Get instant alerts when a listing is mispriced. Built for pro watch dealers.

references.title: 1️⃣ Watch references to track (precise refs only — recommended)
references.description: 🎯 USE PRECISE REFERENCE NUMBERS for accurate arbitrage detection : '5711/1A-010', '116500LN', '124060', '15500ST', '67600ST.OO.1210ST.01'. Mix Patek/Rolex/AP freely (brand auto-detected). 💡 Tip : start with 5-10 references you actually trade — add more later. ⚠️ Model names like 'Nautilus' or 'Daytona' work too but produce LESS PRECISE arbitrage signals (sub-models with different prices get conflated, e.g. men's 5711 vs ladies 7011). Stick to precise references for reliable spread alerts.
references.sectionCaption: ▶ QUICK START — set 3 things and you're done
references.sectionDescription: Just pick the watches you want to monitor and how aggressive the alerts should be. Everything else has smart defaults — skip the advanced section below if you're starting out.

spread_sensitivity.title: 2️⃣ How aggressive should the alerts be? (% — your choice, 0.5 to 50)
spread_sensitivity.description:
Triggers an alert when a listing is BELOW the cross-platform median (or below your per-reference ceiling — see Advanced section) by at least this %. Pick ANY value between 0.5 and 50 — no presets. Lower = more alerts (some noise). Higher = only big opportunities.

Reference: 3% = chatty volume flippers, 5% = recommended default, 8% = selective, 10%+ = once-in-a-week, 15%+ = once-in-a-month. The actor accepts the EXACT number you enter — '4.5' fires alerts at 4.5% spread, not rounded to 5%.

spread_sensitivity_decimal.title: Fine-tune sensitivity (decimal % — 0.5 to 50)
spread_sensitivity_decimal.description: OPTIONAL. If you want sub-integer precision (e.g. '4.5', '7.25', '2.5'), enter it here and it overrides the integer above. Accepted format: a decimal number like '4.5' or '7.25'. Leave blank to use the integer setting.

alert_channel.title: 3️⃣ How do you want to get alerts?
alert_channel.description: Pick your preferred channel. Telegram is fastest for time-sensitive flips. Email digest coming v0.2 (we're verifying delivery permissions on Apify platform first to avoid silently dropped alerts).
enumTitles: 💾 Dataset only (no push alerts — I'll check Apify Console)

alert_telegram_bot_token.title: Telegram bot token
alert_telegram_bot_token.description: 🔧 ONE-TIME setup (5 min): open @BotFather on Telegram → /newbot → name it (e.g. 'My WatchArb Alerts') → copy the token here. Full guide: https://core.telegram.org/bots#how-do-i-create-a-bot
alert_telegram_bot_token.sectionCaption: 📱 Telegram setup (only if you picked Telegram or Both above)
alert_telegram_bot_token.sectionDescription: Skip this section if you picked Email-only or Dataset-only.

alert_telegram_chat_id.title: Your Telegram chat ID
alert_telegram_chat_id.description: 🔧 To find it: open Telegram → search @userinfobot → tap Start → it tells you your numeric chat ID (e.g. '123456789'). Paste it here.

filter_conditions.title: Filter by condition
filter_conditions.description: Only include listings matching these conditions. Critical for arbitrage : a NEW Patek 5711 vs PRE-OWNED can have $30K+ spread (different market). 🎯 PRECISION GUIDE : Bobs Watches + Watchfinder UK + European Watch scrape condition RELIABLY. Chrono24 + WatchBox + Watches of Switzerland often mark 'unknown' (only on detail page). 💡 TIP : if you want STRICT 'new only' results → (a) uncheck 'unknown' below, AND (b) in Marketplaces section uncheck Chrono24/WatchBox/WoS, OR (c) toggle 'Strict condition matching' ON below.
filter_conditions.sectionCaption: 🎚️ Filters (optional — refine which listings count toward arbitrage detection)
filter_conditions.sectionDescription: Filter listings by condition (new/used/vintage) and box-and-papers status. Critical for precise arbitrage : a NEW vs PRE-OWNED 5711 has $30K+ spread.

enumTitles: ✨ Like new / Mint (worn briefly, no signs of wear)
📦 Pre-owned (generic — used by some marketplaces as catch-all)
❓ Unknown (condition not specified on listing — include for completeness)

strict_condition_matching.title: 🔒 Strict condition matching (recommended for 'new only' arbitrage)
strict_condition_matching.description: When ON : exclude listings where condition couldn't be reliably scraped from the search page (= 'unknown'). Pairs perfectly with selecting only Bobs + Watchfinder + European Watch in Marketplaces (those 3 scrape condition reliably). When OFF : include 'unknown' listings in your filter results (more volume, less precision).

filter_box_papers.title: Filter by box & papers
filter_box_papers.description: Only include listings with these box/papers status. Critical for arbitrage : a Patek 5711 'full set' commands +20-30% premium vs 'watch only'. Best-effort : Watchfinder + Bobs + European Watch scrape this reliably ; others may mark 'unknown'. Pair with 'Strict condition matching' for clean filtering.

platforms.title: Marketplaces to monitor
platforms.description: Default: all 6 active marketplaces. Uncheck any you don't want to monitor (e.g. you only flip US-based, uncheck UK/EU sources).

enumTitles: ⚠️ Hodinkee Shop (deprecated — winding down 2026, kept for legacy)

platforms.sectionCaption: ⚙️ Advanced options (everything below is optional — skip if first-time)
platforms.sectionDescription: Defaults work great for most dealers. Tweak only if you have a specific need.

price_ceilings.title: 🎯 Per-reference price ceilings (optional — override the median anchor)
price_ceilings.description:
Set YOUR OWN target intake price per reference, in USD. The alert then fires when ANY platform lists that reference below your ceiling — instead of using the cross-platform median as the anchor.

📝 FORMAT: one row per reference, written as `reference:max_price_usd`. No spaces around the colon. Examples:
  • `5711/1A-010:185000` → alert when 5711/1A-010 is listed below $185,000
  • `116500LN:28000` → alert when 116500LN is listed below $28,000
  • `124060:13500` → alert when 124060 is listed below $13,500

💡 LEAVE EMPTY to keep the default median-anchored behavior for every reference. Mix-and-match supported: refs you list use your ceiling; refs you don't list use the median anchor. Invalid rows (missing colon, non-numeric price, price ≤ 0) are silently skipped — bad rows won't crash the run.

max_listings_per_ref_per_platform.title: Max listings per reference per platform
max_listings_per_ref_per_platform.description: 💰 Cost control: caps how many listings to fetch per ref per marketplace. Lower = cheaper run. Default 25 is good for most cases (= 60 refs × 6 platforms × 25 = 9000 max listings/run).
